description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running lengthwise across its face. The grooves are deep and sharply defined, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, suggesting it is a single, monolithic piece. The coloration is predominantly dark gray to black, with the grooved surfaces showing a slightly lighter, weathered tone. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall form is geometric and modern, suitable for use in public spaces such as sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid sides. The block is dark gray to black, with a robust and modern appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the parallel grooves running away from the viewer.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves on the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with grooves visible but less pronounced due to lighting.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view, again emphasizing the grooved top and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the grooves and surfaces appear clean and well-defined.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
